angles.hook_line: You're paying $3K-$15K/month in SaaS fees. For tools that don't even talk to each other.
angles.visual_direction: Split screen: left shows a messy wall of SaaS logos with price tags adding up. Right shows a single clean dashboard with branding and $0/month. Warm tones, clean composition.
angles.source: gap: no competitor attacks SaaS pricing model directly. {YOUR_COMPANY}'s no-per-user-fee differentiator is uncontested.

copy_variants.primary_text: Every new hire costs you $800-$2K/month in SaaS before they do anything. We build one platform you own — no per-user fees, ever. $5K+/month savings guaranteed.
copy_variants.headline: Stop Renting Your Business Tools

copy_variants.primary_text: A 25-person team was paying $56K/year across 12 different tools. We replaced them all with one custom platform they own. No monthly fees. No per-seat charges. Just theirs.
copy_variants.headline: One Platform. Zero SaaS Fees.

copy_variants.primary_text: How much are you spending on tools your team barely uses? Most SMEs with 10-50 staff waste $20K-$117K/year on disconnected SaaS. What if you owned one system instead?
copy_variants.headline: What's Your SaaS Bill Really?
copy_variants.description: Find out in our free audit

angles.hook_line: We guarantee $5K/month in savings — or you get a full refund plus $5,000.
angles.visual_direction: Bold headline on deep navy (#1B2A4A) background. Electric blue (#3B82F6) accent on the $5K figure. Clean, confident, minimal. No imagery — let the guarantee speak.
angles.source: gap: zero competitors offer ROI guarantees. Every competitor uses vague 'scale faster' language. This is {YOUR_COMPANY}'s strongest differentiator.

inspiration_ads.why: Clean bold text on warm beige, partial rounded rectangle with plant, minimal elements, 704 days active. Adapted layout to navy/blue.
Bold claim + product UI cards on warm background, confident typography. Adapted bold claim approach for guarantee messaging.

copy_variants.primary_text: We audit your operations and guarantee $5K+/month in savings. If we don't find them, full refund plus $5,000 from our pocket. That's how confident we are.
copy_variants.headline: $5K/Month or Your Money Back

copy_variants.primary_text: When we told a client we'd guarantee $5K/month in savings or pay them $5,000, they laughed. Three months later, they'd saved $47K. Nobody else puts money where their mouth is.
copy_variants.headline: We Put $5K On The Line

copy_variants.primary_text: Would your current tech provider guarantee $5K/month in savings — and pay you $5,000 if they failed? We do. For every client. Every time.
copy_variants.headline: Would Your Provider Do This?

images.prompt: Bold graphic advertisement design. Deep navy (#1B2A4A) solid background with subtle dark gradient. Striking visual element: a large glowing shield or guarantee badge rendered in electric blue (#3B82F6) light, floating centre-frame with confident authority.

Bold graphic advertisement design. Deep navy (#1B2A4A) solid background with subtle dark gradient. Striking visual element: a large glowing shield or guarantee badge rendered in electric blue (#3B82F6) light, floating centre-frame with confident authority.

angles.hook_line: Your team is spending 15 hours a week on tasks a machine could do in 15 minutes.
angles.visual_direction: Before/after split. Left: overwhelmed desk with sticky notes, papers, multiple screens open. Right: clean desk, single dashboard, coffee. Warm natural lighting, Australian office setting.
angles.source: competitor pattern: Zapier's proven winners all use aspiration + smooth processes messaging. Adapt for {YOUR_COMPANY}'s custom-build positioning.

inspiration_ads.why: Aspirational text on warm beige, 704 days active. Adapted warm-to-clean transformation visual with before/after graphic design.
Warm beige background, bold headline, minimal graphic elements. Adapted colour approach and composition for before/after split.

copy_variants.primary_text: Your team loses 15-25 hours per person per week to manual data entry, follow-ups, and admin. We build AI systems that handle it automatically. Conservative estimate: $390K/year back.
copy_variants.headline: Get 15 Hours Back Every Week

copy_variants.primary_text: A 40-person trades business was drowning in paperwork. Pen and paper for everything. We built them a digital pipeline — now they're on track from $200K to $1M. Same team, zero extra hires.
copy_variants.headline: Same Team. 5x The Revenue.

copy_variants.primary_text: What would your team do with 15 extra hours every week? Most businesses don't realise how much time they're bleeding to manual processes that AI handles in seconds.
copy_variants.headline: Where Are Your Hours Going?

images.prompt: Split-composition advertisement showing workplace transformation. Before/after split with diagonal divider. Australian office setting.
Split-composition advertisement showing workplace transformation. Before/after split with diagonal divider. Australian office setting.

angles.hook_line: We saved an Australian tradie business $47K/year with one automation.
angles.visual_direction: Clean white card with real numbers. Green success indicators (#10B981). Australian business context — tradies, equipment, real workplaces. No stock photography.
angles.source: gap: no competitor uses Australian social proof. Zapier/Notion are global brands. Kipanga has zero case studies. has 5 real AU case studies.

inspiration_ads.why: UGC tweet-card style, split background, floating social proof card with profile pic. 349 days active. Adapted card format for testimonial/case study.
Dark background with bold headline, AU business automation context. 215 days active. Adapted Australian business credibility positioning.

copy_variants.primary_text: {CASE_STUDY_1}: 40 staff, pen-and-paper to digital pipeline, $200K→$1M trajectory. {CASE_STUDY_2}: eliminated $5-10K errors from manual entry. Real Australian businesses. Real results.
copy_variants.headline: Built For Aussie Businesses

copy_variants.primary_text: A construction company was running 3 different tools and still losing jobs to paperwork errors. We replaced everything with one platform. Field teams went paperless in 2 weeks.
copy_variants.headline: 3 Tools → 1 Platform

copy_variants.primary_text: Would you trust a US chatbot company to understand how an Australian trades business actually runs? Our clients are Australian. Our team is Australian. Our results are Australian.
copy_variants.headline: Your Business Isn't Silicon Valley

images.prompt: Testimonial-style advertisement layout for Australian business results. Clean white card on light grey background with success green indicators.
Testimonial-style advertisement layout for Australian business results. Clean white card on light grey background with success green indicators.

angles.hook_line: Right now, your team has 7 tabs open just to do their job.
angles.visual_direction: Browser mockup showing 7+ tabs (Xero, Monday, HubSpot, Slack, Google Sheets, Calendly, Mailchimp). Then transition to one clean tab: 'Your Business OS'. Modern, relatable.
angles.source: competitor pattern: Zapier's '5,000 apps' angle works because fragmentation is universal. But Zapier adds another tool. replaces them all.

inspiration_ads.why: Stacked Zap cards on warm orange/peach background, product UI showcase style, bold tagline bottom-left. 355 days active. Adapted card layout for disconnected/connected tools concept.
Feature showcase with UI elements, vibrant background, strong offer. Adapted product feature card presentation style.

copy_variants.primary_text: Xero. Monday. HubSpot. Slack. Sheets. Calendly. Mailchimp. That's 7 tools, 7 logins, 7 bills. We replace them with one platform built for your business. You own it forever.
copy_variants.headline: 7 Tools → 1 Platform You Own

copy_variants.primary_text: An agricultural equipment company with 35 staff was running everything on MS Access. Systems breaking at $5M revenue. We rebuilt it all into one platform — now they're scaling past $10M.
copy_variants.headline: Systems Breaking At $5M?

copy_variants.primary_text: How many SaaS logins does your team juggle every day? What if they only needed one — built exactly for how your business works, with AI that actually understands your operations?
copy_variants.headline: One Login. Every Function.

images.prompt: Visual metaphor photograph capturing digital tool overwhelm. Laptop with many browser tabs open. Australian office setting.
Visual metaphor photograph capturing digital tool overwhelm. Laptop with many browser tabs open. Australian office setting.

angles.hook_line: A 10-person team wastes $558,000 a year on inefficiency. We can prove it.
angles.visual_direction: Large $558K figure in bold typography on dark navy background. Breakdown numbers in smaller text below. Clean data visualisation style — authoritative, not flashy.
angles.source: gap: competitors say 'reduce costs' generically. Nobody quantifies the actual waste. has specific ROI framework numbers.

copy_variants.primary_text: SaaS waste: $20K/yr. Manual processes: $390K/yr. Missed AI gains: $148K/yr. Total: $558K/year for a 10-person team. Our $3K audit finds exactly where yours is leaking.
copy_variants.headline: $558K/Year In Hidden Waste

copy_variants.primary_text: When we showed a 25-person company their efficiency numbers, the CFO went quiet. $1.4M in annual waste they didn't know existed. Two sprints later, they'd reclaimed $400K of it.
copy_variants.headline: The Number That Silenced A CFO

copy_variants.primary_text: Do you know how much your business loses to inefficiency every year? For a 10-person team, it's typically $558K. For 50 people? Over $2.8M. We'll calculate your exact number.
copy_variants.headline: What's Your Inefficiency Number?

angles.hook_line: HubSpot charges you per seat. Monday charges you per seat. We build you a system and you own it forever.
angles.visual_direction: Side-by-side comparison table. Left: SaaS column with recurring $ amounts stacking up. Right: column with one-time build cost and $0 ongoing. Success green for savings.
angles.source: gap: no competitor positions ownership vs rental. is the only player offering code ownership with no licensing fees.

copy_variants.primary_text: SaaS companies love recurring revenue — yours. We build custom platforms you own outright. No per-user fees. No annual renewals. Your data, your code, your business. Forever.
copy_variants.headline: Own Your Platform. Not Rent It.

copy_variants.primary_text: An NDIS provider with 50 staff was spending hours daily searching for data across tools they were renting. We built them one system they own — hours reclaimed, every single day.
copy_variants.headline: 50 Staff. Hours Reclaimed Daily.
copy_variants.description: They own every line of code

copy_variants.primary_text: What happens when your SaaS provider raises prices? Or gets acquired? Or shuts down? When you own your platform, those aren't your problems anymore.
copy_variants.headline: What If Your SaaS Shuts Down?
copy_variants.description: Own it. Control it. Scale it.

angles.hook_line: We'll build you one automation for free. No strings. Just to show you what's possible.
angles.visual_direction: Bright, inviting. Electric blue CTA button prominent. Simple illustration of a workflow being automated. Clean white background with brand accents. Approachable, low-pressure.
angles.source: competitor pattern: Notion's $12K free offer is their top performer. Adapt the generous-offer mechanic for {YOUR_COMPANY}'s audit entry point + free automation bonus.

copy_variants.primary_text: Book a $3,000 operational audit and we'll build one automation in week 1 — free. If we don't find $5K+/month in savings, full refund. Audit fee credited 100% if you proceed.
copy_variants.headline: 1 Free Automation. Week 1.

copy_variants.primary_text: We built a client their first automation before the audit was even finished. It saved them 6 hours a week. By the time we delivered the full report, they'd already decided to go all in.
copy_variants.headline: Results Before The Report

copy_variants.primary_text: What if you could see AI working in your business before committing to anything? We build one automation free in the first week of every audit. No strings attached.
copy_variants.headline: See AI Work. Before You Pay.

angles.hook_line: We dropped our agency's scoping process from 200 hours to 20.

image_brief.scene: Bold typographic advertisement. Deep navy (#1B2A4A) background with subtle diagonal gradient to (#162231), faint geometric grid pattern at 3% opacity. Centre-frame: the number '200' rendered large in slate grey (#64748B) with a clean strikethrough line, connected by a sleek rightward arrow in electric blue (#3B82F6) to '20' rendered large and glowing in {YOUR_COMPANY} green (#7DFF00) with a subtle outer glow. Below the numbers: a minimal horizontal timeline bar showing compression — left side stretched (grey), right side compressed (green). Bottom-left: {YOUR_COMPANY} logo (green gear icon, minimum 150px). No people, no stock imagery. The visual should stop scrolling through sheer simplicity and the power of the stat.
image_brief.composition: Numbers centred at 40% height. Timeline bar at 65% height. Bottom 25% clear for headline overlay. logo bottom-left corner, 20px from edges. Strong visual hierarchy: numbers → arrow → timeline → logo.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #162231 gradient target, #64748B for '200' and strikethrough, #3B82F6 for arrow, #7DFF00 for '20' and timeline accent, #FFFFFF for any supporting text
image_brief.style_reference: Match the confident minimal typography and clean background of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/1827916011114841.jpg (Zapier 'Automation isn't magic' — 354-day winner). Adapt the bold-claim-on-clean-background approach with brand colours.
image_brief.text_overlay: No text rendered in image. Clear space reserved at bottom-centre for post-production headline overlay.

scenes.environment: Modern home office with a large ultrawide monitor showing a project management dashboard with timeline bars. Warm afternoon light (4200K) from a window camera-right. Clean desk with a single coffee mug and mechanical keyboard. Bookshelves with tech books out of focus in background. Hardwood floor.
scenes.action: Founder sits at desk, turns from the monitor to face camera. Leans back slightly in chair. Slight eyebrow raise on 'two hundred'. Gestures toward the monitor with right hand on 'twenty'.
scenes.dialogue: We used to spend two hundred hours scoping every new client. Now it takes twenty. — Australian accent, conversational, matter-of-fact tone. Slight emphasis on 'twenty'.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, waist up, slightly off-centre left (rule of thirds). Static for first 2 seconds, then slow push-in over remaining 3 seconds. Shallow depth of field — subject sharp, monitor and bookshelves softly blurred.

scenes.environment: Same office, different angle — now showing the monitor screen more prominently. Screen displays a process map with colour-coded sections and a client portal interface. Late afternoon light has warmed slightly.
scenes.action: Founder stands from chair, walks to the monitor, taps the screen with index finger on a completed process map. Turns back to camera with slight asymmetric smile.
scenes.dialogue: Four AI agents do the extraction, the documentation, and the prototyping. — Same accent, slightly faster pace, building confidence.
scenes.camera: Wide shot transitioning to medium as he stands. Slow pan following his movement to the monitor. Depth of field deepens to show screen content.

scenes.environment: Same office. Monitor now shows a 'one-click install' button interface.
scenes.action: Founder turns fully to camera, slight head tilt, calm authority expression. Holds eye contact.
scenes.dialogue: One-click install. That's it. — Measured, final. Slight pause between sentences.
scenes.camera: Close-up, face and shoulders. Static. Shallow depth of field. Hold for the full 3 seconds.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the product-walkthrough-meets-founder-authority energy of data/competitor-assets/1823602864348394/1328384439046417.mp4 (HighLevel 'What even is HighLevel' — 52-day winner). Clean cuts, professional but not corporate.
video_brief.audio: Subtle ambient office hum. Soft mechanical keyboard clicks in Scene 1. No music — let the dialogue carry.

angles.source: gap: zero competitors use specific transformation metrics. '200→20' is provable, novel, and anchors every other claim.

inspiration_ads.why: 354-day proven winner. Bold claim on clean background, confident typography. Adapt the 'Automation isn't magic' reframing for '200 hours isn't inevitable.'
Dashboard visual with bold claim. Adapt polished brand feel with stat-forward layout.

copy_variants.primary_text: Our agency spent 200 hours scoping every new client. Now it takes 20. Four AI agents extract processes from meeting transcripts, build documentation, and generate clickable prototypes. One-click install.
copy_variants.headline: 200 Hours → 20. Proven.

copy_variants.primary_text: Last quarter we scoped 5 clients simultaneously. Before the AI system, that would've taken 1,000 hours of senior PM time. It took 100. Same quality. Same accuracy. Different century.
copy_variants.headline: We Scoped 5 Clients at Once

copy_variants.primary_text: How many hours does your agency spend scoping each new client? Most spend 150-200. What if you could cut that to 20 — with better accuracy?
copy_variants.headline: What's Your Scoping Cost?

images.prompt: 200 with red strikethrough -> 20 in bold green. Three scoping pipeline cards below (Interviews, Processes, Scope Doc). Warm beige (#FCEFD9) background. logo bottom-right.

angles.hook_line: Scope creep is the silent killer of project margins.

image_brief.scene: Split-screen comparison design on deep navy (#1B2A4A) background. Left half: a stylised chaotic project timeline — overlapping horizontal bars in warning amber (#F59E0B) and muted red (#EF4444), scope arrows expanding outward beyond a dashed boundary line, a small callout badge reading '+60%' in red. Right half: a clean structured timeline — aligned bars in success green (#10B981) and electric blue (#3B82F6), scope contained within solid boundary lines, a small callout badge reading 'On Track' in green. Thin vertical divider line (#64748B) separating the halves. Below both halves centred: subtle text space for headline.
image_brief.composition: Perfect vertical split at 50%. Left half labelled 'Before' in amber at top-left. Right half labelled 'After' in green at top-right. Timelines occupy middle 60% of each half. Bottom 25% clear for headline overlay. logo bottom-right, 20px from edges.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #F59E0B and #EF4444 for 'Before' chaos, #10B981 and #3B82F6 for 'After' order, #64748B for divider and labels, #FFFFFF for callout text
image_brief.style_reference: Match the data-forward professional feel of data/competitor-assets/243382742193644/1308042504498066.jpg (Tech Automation — 53-day winner) but with split-screen comparison layout. Adapt the checklist/feature-list visual clarity.
image_brief.text_overlay: No text rendered in image. Clear space at bottom-centre for post-production headline.

scenes.environment: Bright open-plan agency office. Whiteboard in background with a messy project timeline drawn in red and blue markers, sticky notes scattered around it. Natural daylight from skylights above (5600K). Standing desk with dual monitors, one showing a Slack thread. Plant on windowsill.
scenes.action: Founder stands near the whiteboard, arms crossed, looks at it then turns to camera. Uncrosses arms. Expression shifts from slight frustration to directness on 'silent killer'.
scenes.dialogue: Scope creep is the silent killer of project margins. And it doesn't start in development. — Australian accent, measured, deliberate emphasis on 'silent killer'. Slight pause before second sentence.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, whiteboard visible over left shoulder. Static hold. Shallow depth of field — founder sharp, whiteboard slightly blurred but readable.

scenes.environment: Same agency office but now at the desk. Monitor shows a structured process map with green checkmarks and clearly defined scope boundaries. The whiteboard is now in distant background, out of focus.
scenes.action: Founder walks from whiteboard to desk (movement transition). Sits down, gestures at the monitor showing the AI-generated process map. Slight nod on 'catches what humans miss'. Taps the desk once for emphasis.
scenes.dialogue: It starts in discovery. Missed requirements. Vague documentation. Our AI system catches what humans miss — before a single line of code is written. — Pace picks up slightly, building conviction.
scenes.camera: Follow-shot as he walks, then settles into medium shot at desk. Slow push-in during final sentence. Monitor content visible.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the pain-point storytelling energy of data/competitor-assets/114251326619158/731465073099018.mp4 (BizFlow — 74-day winner). Same AU market, same founder-authority feel but more polished environment.
video_brief.audio: Open office ambient — distant keyboard clicks, muffled conversation. No music.

angles.source: audience language: 'scope creep is the silent killer' is a direct quote from agency forums. No competitor uses this pain in paid ads.

inspiration_ads.why: 74-day winner. Pain-point talking head by software engineer. Same AU market. Adapt the pain-first approach but target scope creep specifically instead of generic ops pain.
74-day winner. Question hook with specific savings claim. Adapt the social-media-question visual hook format.

copy_variants.primary_text: Scope creep doesn't start in development. It starts in discovery. Missed requirements, vague process maps, undocumented assumptions. Our AI scoping system catches what humans miss — before a single line of code is written.
copy_variants.headline: Kill Scope Creep at the Source

copy_variants.primary_text: A $120K project blew out to $180K because three critical processes were missed during scoping. The client was furious. The margin evaporated. We built an AI system so that never happens again.
copy_variants.headline: The $60K Scoping Mistake

copy_variants.primary_text: When was the last time a project blew its budget because something was missed in scoping? For most agencies, it's every quarter. What if your discovery process caught everything?
copy_variants.headline: Missing Requirements Cost You

images.prompt: SCOPE in massive white bold with red (#DC2626) explosion arrows radiating outward. is eating your margins in grey below. Dark navy-grey (#1E293B) background. logo bottom-right.

angles.hook_line: What if your junior PM could deliver the same quality scopes as your technical director?

image_brief.scene: Professional empowerment photograph. Young woman (late-20s) at a modern standing desk in a light-filled co-working space. She's looking at a large monitor displaying a clean AI dashboard with structured process maps in electric blue (#3B82F6) and green (#7DFF00). Natural warm light (4500K) from floor-to-ceiling windows camera-left. Indoor plants in background, exposed timber beams. She has a slight confident smile, one hand resting on the desk.
image_brief.composition: Subject at left third line, facing right toward the monitor. Monitor content visible at right third. Bottom 20% clear for headline. Top-right corner: logo. Environmental context fills the frame — this isn't isolated, it's a real workspace.
image_brief.colour_palette: #FFFFFF and warm naturals for the environment, #3B82F6 and #7DFF00 on the monitor UI elements, #1B2A4A for any dark UI elements on screen
image_brief.style_reference: Match the aspirational-professional feel of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/416986901046052.jpg (Zapier 'level up' — 704-day winner) but with a person in a real workspace rather than pure graphic.
image_brief.text_overlay: No text rendered in image. Clear space at bottom-left for post-production headline.

scenes.environment: Modern co-working space with floor-to-ceiling windows, warm morning light (4500K) streaming from camera-left. Long communal timber table with scattered laptops. Indoor plants — a fiddle-leaf fig in the corner. Exposed brick accent wall. A MacBook Pro open on the table with headphones beside it.
scenes.action: PM character sits at the communal table, closes her laptop lid confidently, picks up a notebook. Slight bright smile on 'solo'. Tucks hair behind left ear as she stands.
scenes.dialogue: I used to sit in on every discovery session just to take notes. Now I run them solo. — Australian accent, clear, professional. Tone: quiet confidence, not boastful.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, waist up at the table. Natural framing with depth — other empty chairs visible. Static hold with slight drift right as she stands.

scenes.environment: Small glass-walled meeting room adjacent to the co-working space. Standing whiteboard with a freshly drawn process diagram. Overhead pendant light (warm 3500K). A client's laptop open on the small table. Two coffee cups.
scenes.action: PM stands at the whiteboard, gestures at the process diagram with a marker in hand. Turns to look at (implied) client across the table. Slight head tilt on 'couldn't fault'. Sets the marker down on the table deliberately.
scenes.dialogue: The AI captures everything, structures it, and generates deliverables my technical director couldn't fault. — Pace: measured, building. Emphasis on 'everything' and 'couldn't fault'. Slight eyebrow raise on 'couldn't fault'.
scenes.camera: Wide shot showing the full meeting room context, then slow push-in to medium shot. Whiteboard content visible. Shallow depth of field on final frame — PM sharp, whiteboard slightly soft.

scenes.environment: Back at the co-working space communal table. Her MacBook now shows a completed client portal with multiple sections. Afternoon light has shifted warmer (4000K).
scenes.action: PM sits back down, opens the MacBook, turns it to face camera showing the portal. Confident direct eye contact with slight smile. Hands rest calmly on the table.
scenes.dialogue: She's back to building. And I deliver senior-quality scopes. — Slower pace, final statement energy. Genuine warmth.
scenes.camera: Close-up transitioning to medium as she turns the laptop. Hold on final frame — her face and the laptop screen both visible. Static.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the personal-confession scaling story energy of data/competitor-assets/114251326619158/872327002382193.mp4 (BizFlow 'I want to scale' — 63-day winner). Same authentic, un-scripted feel but with the PM character instead of a founder.
video_brief.audio: Co-working ambient — distant conversation murmur, espresso machine in background in Scene 1. Meeting room quieter in Scene 2 — just pen on whiteboard. Scene 3: subtle laptop fan hum. No music.

angles.source: market research: agencies resist 'replace X FTEs' messaging — they want to empower existing team. 'Replace the process, not the person' resonates.

inspiration_ads.why: 63-day winner. 'I want to scale but don't have capacity.' Same scaling pain. Adapt the personal-confession format for the empowerment angle.
52-day winner. Explainer-style targeting agencies. Adapt the 'this is how it works' walkthrough format.

copy_variants.primary_text: Your junior PM runs the client meeting. The AI extracts every process, pain point, and requirement. It builds the documentation, the process maps, and the prototype. Senior quality output. Junior cost.
copy_variants.headline: Empower Your Whole Team

copy_variants.primary_text: We used to need our CTO in every discovery session. Now our project coordinator runs them solo. The AI captures everything, structures it, and generates deliverables our CTO couldn't fault. She's back to building.
copy_variants.headline: Free Your Senior Staff

copy_variants.primary_text: How much of your senior team's time is spent documenting processes instead of building? What if anyone on your team could run a discovery session and produce expert-level scopes?
copy_variants.headline: Who Runs Your Discoveries?

images.prompt: Diagonal split. Left: muted, cluttered desk, scattered docs, Junior PM label in grey. Right: clean laptop with process map, Senior Scopes label in bold dark text. Warm vs cool contrast. logo bottom-right.

angles.hook_line: Every new client needs your best person in the room. What happens when you have 5 at once?

image_brief.scene: Abstract architectural visual metaphor on deep navy (#1B2A4A) background. One glowing silhouette figure (electric blue #3B82F6 outline, no facial detail) at centre-left, with 5 parallel horizontal lanes extending rightward from the figure. Each lane is a different subtle colour tint (all palette) and terminates in a small glowing portal/screen icon representing a different client engagement. The lanes have gentle perspective — narrower at the figure, wider at the portals. Subtle particle effects along the lanes suggesting data flow. The figure stands at a junction point — one person, five simultaneous paths.
image_brief.composition: Figure at 30% from left, lanes extend to 90% right. Lanes occupy middle 50% of vertical space. Bottom 25% clear for headline. Top-left: logo. Clean negative space above and below the lanes.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #3B82F6 for the figure outline and primary lane, #7DFF00 #10B981 #F59E0B #64748B for the other four lanes (subtle tints), #FFFFFF for portal icons
image_brief.style_reference: Match the aspirational-clean composition of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/3328979794067876.jpg (Zapier 'smooth processes' — 704-day winner). Adapt the sense of flow and organisational scaling but with a more abstract, architectural approach.
image_brief.text_overlay: No text rendered in image. Clear space at bottom-centre for post-production headline.

scenes.environment: Agency boardroom. Long timber conference table with 5 open laptops at different seats, each showing a different client's project. Floor-to-ceiling glass wall looking out onto a city skyline at golden hour (3800K). Pendant lights overhead. Architectural feel — clean lines, premium materials.
scenes.action: Founder walks slowly along the length of the table, trailing his hand along the back of each chair as he passes. Glances at each laptop screen. Slight concerned expression on 'best person'. Stops at the head of the table.
scenes.dialogue: Every new client engagement requires your best person in the room. — Australian accent, measured, slightly rhetorical. Walking pace matches the deliberate delivery.
scenes.camera: Tracking shot following the founder along the table from left to right. Medium-wide, showing him and the laptops. Steady dolly. Golden hour light catches his face intermittently.

scenes.environment: Same boardroom, now at the head of the table. All 5 laptops visible from his perspective. City skyline deeper in golden hour. Warm light pools on the timber table.
scenes.action: Founder places both hands on the table, leans forward slightly. Slight head tilt and eyebrow micro-raise on 'five at once'. Straightens up with calm authority for the transition.
scenes.dialogue: What happens when you have five at once? — Pause after 'five'. Tone shifts from question to quiet confidence. Direct eye contact with camera.
scenes.camera: Reverse angle — now facing the founder from across the table, laptops in foreground blurred. Close-up on final frame. Static hold with very subtle push-in.

scenes.environment: Same boardroom. Founder now at a single laptop at the head of the table. Screen shows a dashboard with 5 active client engagements running simultaneously — each with progress bars and process map thumbnails.
scenes.action: Founder sits down, opens the laptop, scrolls through the 5 concurrent engagements. Slight asymmetric smile on 'same quality'. Looks up at camera for the final line.
scenes.dialogue: One system. Five discoveries running simultaneously. Same quality every time. — Confident, conclusive. Emphasis on 'five' and 'every time'.
scenes.camera: Over-shoulder shot showing the laptop screen, then cut to close-up of his face for the final line. Static on the close-up.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the aspirational scaling energy of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/3328979794067876.jpg (Zapier 'smooth processes') combined with the founder authority of data/competitor-assets/114251326619158/731465073099018.mp4 (BizFlow founder — 74-day winner).
video_brief.audio: Quiet boardroom ambience — distant city hum through glass, soft footsteps on carpet in Scene 1. No music. Let the space and dialogue breathe.

angles.source: market theme: agency capacity ceiling — founder/senior PM is the bottleneck on every new engagement. No competitor addresses this.

inspiration_ads.why: 704-day proven winner. 'Smooth processes for all.' Aspiration-led carousel about organisational scaling. Adapt the scaling aspiration for agency-specific discovery bottleneck.
704-day proven winner. 'Want to level up your company?' Curiosity hook. Adapt the question-led scaling approach.

copy_variants.primary_text: You can't clone your founder. But you can give every team member a scoping system that captures, structures, and documents like your best person would. Run 5 discoveries simultaneously without the bottleneck.
copy_variants.headline: Scale Without the Bottleneck

copy_variants.primary_text: We used to turn away clients because we couldn't scope fast enough. The founder had to be in every room. Now we run 5 client engagements at once — same quality, consistent output, zero dependence on one person.
copy_variants.headline: We Stopped Turning Away Work

copy_variants.primary_text: How many potential clients did you turn away this quarter because your senior team was maxed out on discovery? What if that wasn't a constraint anymore?
copy_variants.headline: How Many Clients Did You Lose?

images.prompt: 1 Founder with red strikethrough -> Whole Team bold below. Warm beige (#F8EFEB) background. Partial rounded rectangle on right showing agency whiteboard with sticky notes process flow + laptop. Coral (#F4926C) accent. logo top-left.

videos.prompt: STYLE: Shot on Sony FX3, 35mm f/1.4 Sigma Art lens. Natural colour grade, warm skin tones, slightly desaturated highlights. 3 scenes (3s+3s+2s) — boardroom tracking dolly, reverse angle push-in, close-up hold. Dialogue: Every new client engagement requires your best person in the room. What happens when you have five at once? One system. Five discoveries. Same quality every time.

angles.angle_name: We Run 5 Audits a Month on This
angles.hook_line: Our agency runs 5 client engagements a month using this exact system. Here's what the output looks like.

image_brief.scene: Screen-capture style product showcase. Dark UI background (#0F1923) showing a tiled view of 4 real-looking deliverables: top-left is a process map with colour-coded sections (blues and greens), top-right is a client portal with progressive unlock sections, bottom-left is a clickable prototype with UI components, bottom-right is a strategic analysis with a bubble chart. Each tile has a subtle glow border in electric blue (#3B82F6). The overall feel is 'looking at a real product dashboard' — authentic, not mocked up.
image_brief.composition: 2x2 grid of deliverable tiles centred, each tile occupying about 40% width with 5% gutters. Tiles occupy top 70% of frame. Bottom 25% clear for headline on navy (#1B2A4A) bar. logo bottom-right.
image_brief.colour_palette: #0F1923 UI background, #1B2A4A headline bar, #3B82F6 tile glow borders, #7DFF00 accents within the deliverable UIs, #10B981 for success indicators within tiles, #FFFFFF for text within tiles
image_brief.style_reference: Match the dashboard-showcase approach of data/competitor-assets/1823602864348394/934774155561164.jpg (HighLevel — 53-day winner) with feature callout pills. Adapt for showing real deliverable outputs rather than a SaaS dashboard.
image_brief.text_overlay: No text rendered in image. Clear space at bottom-centre for post-production headline.

scenes.environment: Home office with character — warm, lived-in but tidy. Large ultrawide monitor on a sit-stand desk (currently standing height). Monitor shows a client portal with multiple completed sections. Warm desk lamp (3200K) providing key light from camera-left. Shelving unit behind with a mix of tech books and a small plant. Dark wall behind shelving.
scenes.action: Founder stands at the desk, coffee mug in left hand (takes a sip then sets it down). Turns from the monitor to face camera. Direct eye contact. Slight nod on 'exact system'.
scenes.dialogue: Our agency runs five client engagements a month using this exact system. This isn't a concept — let me show you what the output looks like. — Australian accent, conversational, direct. Slight emphasis on 'five' and 'exact system'. Tone: pride without arrogance.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, standing at desk height. Monitor partially visible over right shoulder. Static for the first 2 seconds, then slow push-in to medium-close on 'let me show you'.

scenes.environment: Screen recording transition — the monitor fills the frame. Shows a real-looking process map with colour-coded waste heatmap overlays (reds for high waste, greens for efficient). The map has connected nodes, department labels, and time estimates. Then transitions to a client portal showing progressive sections: Findings, Solutions, Strategic Approaches (some locked, some unlocked).
scenes.action: Cursor moves naturally across the screen — clicks on a process node to expand it, scrolls down the portal to show the progressive sections. Movement is natural, not robotic — slight mouse drift, realistic scroll speed.
scenes.dialogue: Process maps generated from meeting transcripts. Client portals with findings, solutions, and strategies — all structured automatically. — Voice continues as voiceover during screen recording. Measured, explanatory.
scenes.camera: Full-screen capture. Slight slow zoom into the portal section during the second sentence. Clean transitions between the two views (quick dissolve).

scenes.environment: Back to the home office. Founder has moved to a nearby armchair (leather, warm brown). Laptop on his knee showing a clickable prototype. Warmer evening light now (3000K) — desk lamp still on but natural light has faded.
scenes.action: Founder sits in the armchair, laptop on knee, gestures at the screen with right hand. Looks up from the laptop to camera. Slight asymmetric smile. Sets the laptop aside on the armrest.
scenes.dialogue: Even clickable prototypes. Built from the requirements the AI extracted. Try the demo yourself. — Final sentence is an invitation, slightly warmer tone. Direct eye contact.
scenes.camera: Medium-wide shot showing the armchair context, then cut to close-up for 'Try the demo yourself'. Hold on the close-up.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the product-walkthrough credibility of data/competitor-assets/1823602864348394/1328384439046417.mp4 (HighLevel explainer — 52-day winner) combined with the founder authenticity of data/competitor-assets/114251326619158/731465073099018.mp4 (BizFlow — 74-day winner). Screen recording section should feel like a genuine product demo, not a marketing animation.
video_brief.audio: Scene 1: Home office ambient — slight desk fan hum, coffee mug clink. Scene 2: Quiet — just subtle mouse clicks during screen recording. Scene 3: Leather chair creak as he sits back. No music throughout.

angles.source: gap: zero competitor winners use specific metrics or real results. Adam's agency credibility + YouTube presence + real deliverables is uncontested proof.

inspiration_ads.why: 52-day winner. 'What even is HighLevel?' Explainer format showing real product. Adapt the product walkthrough approach with real scoping deliverables.
74-day winner. Founder talking head with credibility. Adapt the 'I built this and use it myself' founder authority.

copy_variants.primary_text: This isn't a concept. We've run 5 client scoping engagements this month on this system. Process maps, findings reports, strategic approaches, clickable prototypes — all generated from meeting transcripts. Try the interactive demo.
copy_variants.headline: See the Real Output

copy_variants.primary_text: I built this system because I was sick of spending 200 hours scoping every client. Now my agency runs 5 concurrent discoveries with consistent, polished output. I'm making it available to other agencies.
copy_variants.headline: Built by an Agency. For Agencies.

copy_variants.primary_text: What would your clients say if you handed them a clickable prototype, process map, and strategic analysis — all built from their meeting transcripts? Our clients can't believe the turnaround.
copy_variants.headline: What If Scoping Wowed Clients?

images.prompt: 5 audits in massive white bold, a month in electric blue (#3B82F6) below. Laptop showing process map with fanned deliverable documents surrounding it. Deep navy (#1B2A4A) background. logo top-left.

angles.angle_name: Not a Spreadsheet. A Scoping Engine.
angles.hook_line: Not a spreadsheet. Not a template. A scoping engine that thinks.

image_brief.scene: Bold typographic reframe advertisement. Dark charcoal background (#1B2A4A) with very subtle noise texture at 2% opacity. Three lines of text stacked vertically, left-aligned at 15% from left edge. Line 1: 'Not a spreadsheet.' in medium slate grey (#64748B) with a clean horizontal strikethrough line in the same grey. Line 2: 'Not a template.' same treatment — grey text, grey strikethrough. Line 3: 'A Scoping Engine.' in bright white (#FFFFFF), large bold, with a short thick underline accent in {YOUR_COMPANY} green (#7DFF00). Below the three lines, a subtle horizontal divider, then 4 small icon-and-text feature rows in white at 60% opacity: '→ Extracts from meeting transcripts', '→ Builds process maps', '→ Generates prototypes', '→ One-click install'. logo top-left corner.
image_brief.composition: Text left-aligned, occupying left 65% of frame. Right 35% is intentional negative space (navy background breathes). Text starts at 20% from top. Feature rows end at 75% from top. Bottom 20% clear for headline overlay. Strong typographic hierarchy: strikethrough lines draw eye, then the white 'A Scoping Engine.' pops.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #64748B for struck-through text and divider, #FFFFFF for hero text and feature rows, #7DFF00 for underline accent
image_brief.style_reference: Match the bold reframing layout of data/competitor-assets/688036534387584/931038652718356.jpg (Northstar 'Not a chatbot. An employee.' — 35-day winner). Same dark background, same crossout-and-replace pattern, same feature-list-below structure. Also inspired by data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/1827916011114841.jpg (Zapier 'isn't magic' — confident typography).
image_brief.text_overlay: All text is part of the image design (not post-production overlay). The strikethrough text, hero text, and feature rows ARE the creative. No additional overlay needed.

scenes.environment: Clean white desk in a bright studio-like space. A spreadsheet printout and a Notion-style template printout are physically on the desk. Natural diffused light (5000K) from above — clean, editorial feel. Minimalist background — white wall with a single framed abstract print.
scenes.action: Founder sits at the desk, picks up the spreadsheet printout, looks at it briefly, then places it face-down with a slight dismissive gesture. Does the same with the template. Then opens a laptop showing the AI scoping engine interface. Slight head shake on 'tried everything'. Decisive expression when placing papers down.
scenes.dialogue: We tried every scoping template. Every spreadsheet. Every Notion board. — Pause — None of them worked at scale. So we built something different. — Australian accent, conversational. The first three sentences are rhythmic (same cadence). The final sentence shifts — quieter, more deliberate.
scenes.camera: Close-up on hands and papers for first 3 seconds (don't show face yet — build intrigue). Cut to medium shot revealing the founder for the final sentence. Shallow depth of field.

scenes.environment: Same desk. Laptop now prominent, showing the scoping engine dashboard with 4 AI agent status indicators.
scenes.action: Founder turns the laptop to face camera slightly. Taps the screen once. Direct eye contact. Calm authority expression.
scenes.dialogue: Four AI agents. One pipeline. It just works. — Short, punchy. Each phrase is its own beat.
scenes.camera: Over-shoulder showing laptop screen, then quick cut to close-up of face for 'It just works'. Static hold.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the de-mystification energy of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/1827916011114841.jpg (Zapier 'isn't magic') adapted to video. Clean, editorial, objects-tell-the-story.
video_brief.audio: Paper rustling when picking up printouts. Laptop hinge sound when opening. Minimal ambient — studio quiet. No music.

angles.source: competitor adaptation: Northstar Digital's 'Not a chatbot. An employee.' bold reframing (35-day winner). Adapt the pattern for scoping tools.

inspiration_ads.why: 35-day winner. 'Not a chatbot. An employee.' Bold reframing with text-heavy overlay. Directly adaptable layout — cross out the old thing, name the new thing.
354-day winner. 'Automation isn't magic. It's Zapier.' Same reframing structure. Confident typography on clean background.

copy_variants.primary_text: Spreadsheets don't extract requirements from meeting transcripts. Templates don't build clickable prototypes. You need a system that turns client conversations into structured deliverables. That's what this is.
copy_variants.headline: A Scoping Engine. Not a Tool.

copy_variants.primary_text: We tried every scoping template, every requirements doc format, every Notion board. Nothing worked at scale. So we built 4 AI agents that do the extraction, analysis, and deliverable generation. Now we sell the system.
copy_variants.headline: We Tried Everything Else First

copy_variants.primary_text: Are you still scoping projects with spreadsheets and Google Docs? What if 4 AI agents could extract, analyse, and document everything from your meeting transcripts automatically?
copy_variants.headline: Still Using Spreadsheets?

images.prompt: Two-tone split 9:16 ad. Top 75% light grey (#E8E8E8): logo top-centre, bold black 'Spreadsheet' with orange (#F86E20) strikethrough, 'AI Scoping Engine.' bold below, 'Yours. Forever.' tagline, four orange checkmark benefit rows. Bottom 25% dark charcoal (#262626): large rounded orange (#F86E20) CTA button with bold white 'Learn More' text. Regenerated to fix empty CTA button.

angles.angle_name: Pays for Itself in Week One
angles.hook_line: One full-time PM costs you $8K/month. This system costs $5K once.

image_brief.scene: ROI comparison infographic design on deep navy (#1B2A4A) background. Two vertical columns with a thin divider (#64748B) between them. Left column: header 'Full-Time PM' in warning amber (#F59E0B), large price '$8K/month' in amber, subtext 'Every month. Forever.' in slate grey (#64748B) at 50% opacity. Right column: header 'AI Scoping Engine' in {YOUR_COMPANY} green (#7DFF00), large price '$5K once' in green, subtext 'You own it forever.' in white (#FFFFFF) at 80% opacity. Below both columns: a horizontal payback timeline bar — a thin track in slate grey with a green progress fill stopping at approximately 15% (representing ~2 weeks out of the first quarter). Label below the bar: 'Payback: ~2 weeks' in white. logo bottom-left.
image_brief.composition: Columns occupy top 55% of frame, evenly split. Payback bar at 65% height. Bottom 25% clear for headline overlay. Symmetrical layout — the comparison IS the visual. Clean data presentation, no decorative elements.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #F59E0B for PM column header and price, #7DFF00 for AI column header and price, #64748B for divider and subtext, #FFFFFF for supporting text and payback label, #10B981 for payback bar fill
image_brief.style_reference: Match the AU-targeted data-forward comparison feel of data/competitor-assets/243382742193644/1308042504498066.jpg (Tech Automation — 53-day winner). Clean checklist/comparison layout adapted for a cost comparison. Also draw from data/competitor-assets/1823602864348394/934774155561164.jpg (HighLevel dashboard) for the polished brand authority.
image_brief.text_overlay: The prices and labels are part of the image design. No additional post-production text needed except the headline in the bottom clear space.

scenes.environment: Modern agency office — glass-walled meeting room visible in background with a team working. Founder at a standing desk with a large monitor showing a hiring platform (Seek/Indeed style). Bright office lighting (5000K), commercial fit-out feel. Whiteboard on the wall with 'PM Hire — $95K + super' written on it.
scenes.action: Founder leans on the standing desk, scrolls the hiring platform on the monitor. Turns to camera with a 'let me tell you something' expression — slight forward lean, one hand on desk. Gestures toward the whiteboard with his other hand on 'ninety-five K'.
scenes.dialogue: We were about to hire a second PM at ninety-five K a year. — Australian accent, conversational, 'can you believe it' energy. Not dismissive of PMs — just illustrating the cost.
scenes.camera: Medium-wide shot establishing the office context. Whiteboard and monitor both visible. Static hold. Slight depth — team working in background glass room provides life.

scenes.environment: Same office, now at a different desk area. Monitor shows the AI scoping engine pricing page — '$5,000 one-time'. Clean desk with just the monitor and a notebook.
scenes.action: Founder walks from the standing desk to this cleaner desk (movement transition). Sits down on an office chair, spins slightly to face the monitor. Points at the price with a pen. Turns back to camera with slight asymmetric smile on 'never happened'.
scenes.dialogue: Instead we installed this for five K. One time. That hire? Never happened. — Pace shifts — faster on 'five K, one time', then slows for emphasis on 'never happened'. Slight conspiratorial tone.
scenes.camera: Follow-shot during walk, then settles into medium shot at the clean desk. Slow push-in during 'never happened'. Monitor price visible.

scenes.environment: Same office. Founder now standing, looking out the glass wall at the team working. Reflective, confident posture.
scenes.action: Founder stands at the glass wall, hands in pockets, watches the team briefly. Turns to camera. Calm authority. Slight nod.
scenes.dialogue: The savings compound every single month. — Quiet, conclusive. Let it land.
scenes.camera: Wide shot showing the office context and team through glass, then cut to close-up for the final line. Hold.

video_brief.style_reference: Match the relatable founder-story energy of data/competitor-assets/114251326619158/1533200171245371.mp4 (BizFlow question hook with savings claim — 74-day winner). Same 'I solved this problem, let me show you' vibe.
video_brief.audio: Office ambient — air conditioning hum, distant keyboard clicks, muffled team conversation through glass. Chair rolling sound in Scene 2. No music.

angles.source: market theme: margin pressure — discovery/scoping eating 15-25% of project budget. No competitor makes the PM cost comparison explicit.

inspiration_ads.why: 53-day winner. AU-targeted feature comparison with specific benefits. Adapt the comparison visual format for PM cost vs system cost.
53-day winner. Dashboard + bold claim. Adapt the data-forward polished brand layout for the ROI comparison.

copy_variants.primary_text: A senior PM costs $8K-$12K/month. This AI scoping system costs $5K once. It does the extraction, documentation, and deliverable generation. You keep the PM for judgment calls. ROI in week one.
copy_variants.headline: $5K Once. Not $8K/Month.

copy_variants.primary_text: We were about to hire a second PM at $95K/year. Instead, we installed the AI scoping system for $5K. It handles the documentation workload of 1.5 PMs. That hire never happened. The savings compound every month.
copy_variants.headline: The Hire We Never Made

copy_variants.primary_text: What does your agency spend on scoping each year? Most spend $50K-$150K in PM time on pre-build discovery alone. What if you could cut that by 90% with a one-time $5K investment?
copy_variants.headline: What's Your Scoping Budget?

images.prompt: $8K/mo with green strikethrough -> $5K once in bold green. Dark blue (#0F255A) background. Founder character gesturing toward comparison. Five green checkmarks. logo top-left.

angles.hook_line: This AI doesn't chat. It ships.
angles.source: gap: every competitor uses generic AI language ("AI-powered", "smart automation"). None differentiate from ChatGPT. This reframes AI as a delivery tool, not a chatbot.

copy_variants.primary_text: ChatGPT writes emails. Our AI reads your client interviews, maps your processes, and delivers scope documents. It doesn't chat — it ships real deliverables your team would take weeks to produce.
copy_variants.headline: AI That Ships, Not Chats

copy_variants.primary_text: We gave our AI 47 client meeting transcripts last month. It analysed every one, mapped the processes, and produced scope documents. No prompting. No back-and-forth. Just deliverables, shipped.
copy_variants.headline: This AI Doesn't Chat

copy_variants.primary_text: Your team spent last week writing scope docs by hand. What if an AI could read your client meetings and ship those docs in hours? Not a chatbot. A delivery engine.
copy_variants.headline: What If AI Actually Delivered?
copy_variants.description: Not a chatbot — a scoping engine

images.prompt: Chatbot with orange strikethrough -> AI That Ships. bold below. Three arrows: Reads your meetings, Maps your processes, Ships scope docs. Two-tone grey/charcoal layout with orange CTA. logo top.

angles.hook_line: 3 analyst salaries. Or one AI system for $5K.
angles.source: gap: no competitor makes the AI-vs-headcount cost comparison explicit. Agencies understand headcount pressure — this makes the ROI visceral.

copy_variants.primary_text: A business analyst costs $85K/year. A process mapper costs $90K/year. A scope writer costs $80K/year. Or you get one AI system that does all three — for $5K once. You own it forever.
copy_variants.headline: $255K in Salaries or $5K Once

copy_variants.primary_text: An agency owner told us he needed 3 new hires to handle scoping for his growing client base. We showed him one AI system that analyses interviews, maps processes, and writes scope docs. Cost: $5K. He cancelled the job ads.
copy_variants.headline: 3 Job Ads Cancelled
copy_variants.description: One AI system replaced them all

copy_variants.primary_text: How much would you pay for an employee that reads every client meeting, maps every process, and writes every scope doc — without sick days, without training, without a salary? $5K. Once.
copy_variants.headline: What Would You Pay for This?

images.prompt: $255K with green strikethrough + 3 analyst salaries context -> $5K once in bold green + one AI system. Three checkmarks: Reads every client meeting, Maps every process, Writes every scope doc. Founder character. Dark blue background. logo top-left.

angles.hook_line: How much is your scoping process actually costing you? Most agencies have no idea.

image_brief.scene: Clean calculator interface showing inputs: 3 PMs × $85K salary × 40% time on scoping = large annual number revealed. Dark navy (#1B2A4A) background. Calculator is stylised, not a literal app screenshot.
image_brief.composition: Calculator centered, result number large and prominent at bottom. logo bottom-right. Space reserved top-left for hook text overlay.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #F59E0B amber for the calculated number, #FFFFFF for input labels, #3B82F6 for accent lines
image_brief.style_reference: Match clean data presentation of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/1827916011114841.jpg — simple, authoritative, one focal number
image_brief.text_overlay: $127K/yr in small amber text at the calculated result position — max 12 chars

scenes.environment: Modern co-working space, warm afternoon light from camera-left, standing desk with laptop showing abstract soft colour blocks on screen, coffee mug on desk.
scenes.action: Walks into frame from right, stops at desk, picks up coffee mug, looks directly at camera with slight head tilt.
scenes.dialogue: Quick question. How many project managers do you have?
scenes.camera: Medium shot, static, shallow depth of field on face

scenes.environment: Same location, tighter framing. Laptop screen shows abstract warm-toned gradient blocks.
scenes.action: Sets coffee down, leans forward slightly, counts on fingers with slight asymmetric smile.
scenes.dialogue: Multiply their salary by the percentage of time they spend on scoping. That's your number.
scenes.camera: Close-up, slow push-in, shallow depth of field

scenes.environment: Same location, pulls back to medium-wide.
scenes.action: Straightens up, calm authority expression, slight nod.
scenes.dialogue: We cut that number by ninety percent. Want to see yours?
scenes.camera: Medium shot, static hold, direct eye contact

video_brief.style_reference: Match conversational energy of BizFlow pain-point videos. Shot on Sony FX3, 35mm f/1.4 Sigma Art. Natural colour grade, warm skin tones.
video_brief.audio: Subtle office ambient — keyboard clicks, air conditioning hum. No music.

angles.source: Hormozi value equation: make the dream outcome concrete by calculating THEIR specific number. ROI calculator as mid-funnel qualification — activation opportunity #7 from market intel.

inspiration_ads.why: Clean single-metric visual presentation. One authoritative number as focal point. Warm palette. Adapt the 'one big number' layout for ROI calculator hook.

copy_variants.primary_text: 3 PMs × 40% of their week on scoping = a number that'll make you uncomfortable. We built an AI system that cuts scoping time by 90%. Plug in your team size and see what you'll save.
copy_variants.headline: Calculate Your Scoping Waste

copy_variants.primary_text: We ran the numbers on our own agency. 200 hours of manual scoping per engagement. When we saw the annual cost, we built something to fix it. Now we're letting other agencies run their own numbers.
copy_variants.headline: What's Your Scoping Costing?

copy_variants.primary_text: What if you could see exactly how much your manual scoping process costs per year? Not a guess — your team size, your rates, your hours. Most agency owners are shocked by the number.
copy_variants.headline: See Your Real Number

angles.angle_name: The Discovery Call That Pays for Itself
angles.hook_line: Book a 30-minute call. We'll show you the exact dollar amount your scoping process is wasting.

image_brief.scene: Split layout: left side shows a calendar booking interface (abstract, no readable text), right side shows a large dollar figure with a downward arrow indicating savings. Clean, professional.
image_brief.composition: 60/40 split. Calendar on left (muted), savings number on right (bright, prominent). logo top-left. CTA space reserved bottom-centre.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background left side, #FFFFFF right side, #10B981 for savings number, #3B82F6 for CTA accent
image_brief.style_reference: Match the two-tone split layout of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/3328979794067876.jpg — warm/cool contrast, clean division
image_brief.text_overlay: 30 min in white on the calendar side — max 6 chars

scenes.environment: Bright meeting room, whiteboard in background with abstract shapes (no readable text). Natural light from large window. Founder seated at head of table.
scenes.action: Leans forward in chair, hands clasped on table, direct eye contact, calm confidence.
scenes.dialogue: Here's what I'll do on our call. I'll calculate exactly what your scoping process costs per year.
scenes.camera: Medium close-up, static, shallow depth of field

scenes.environment: Same room, wider angle showing the whiteboard with abstract warm-toned shapes.
scenes.action: Stands up, walks toward camera, gestures with right hand on the word 'system'.
scenes.dialogue: Then I'll show you the system our agency uses to cut that number by ninety percent.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, slight tracking as he stands, depth of field shifts to follow

scenes.environment: Back at desk, closer. Laptop with abstract gradient on screen.
scenes.action: Slight asymmetric smile, nod, relaxed posture.
scenes.dialogue: No pitch. Just your numbers. Book below.
scenes.camera: Close-up, static hold

video_brief.style_reference: Shot on Sony FX3, 50mm f/1.4. Slightly warmer grade than scene 1. Confident, low-pressure energy — like explaining to a smart friend.
video_brief.audio: Room tone only — no music, no effects.

angles.source: Hormozi: reduce perceived effort/sacrifice by making the first step free and low-commitment. 'No pitch, just your numbers' eliminates sales resistance. Discovery call as the conversion event.

inspiration_ads.why: Two-tone warm/cool split layout. Clean division between problem and solution. Carousel format for multi-step value reveal. Adapt the warm palette and split composition.

copy_variants.primary_text: 30 minutes. I'll calculate your scoping costs, show you the system we use to run 5 client engagements simultaneously, and give you the numbers. No pitch — just data. Book below.
copy_variants.headline: Your Numbers. 30 Minutes.

copy_variants.primary_text: Last week, an agency founder booked a call expecting a sales pitch. Instead, I showed him his team was spending $140K/year on manual scoping. He didn't need convincing after that.
copy_variants.headline: See What You're Spending

copy_variants.primary_text: What if a 30-minute call could show you exactly where $50K-$200K is leaking from your agency every year? No sales pitch. Just your team size, your rates, and the math.
copy_variants.headline: One Call. Your Real Numbers.

angles.hook_line: Your next project manager costs $85K. This system replaces the scoping half of their job.

image_brief.scene: Clean comparison layout. Left column: PM salary breakdown ($85K, with 40% highlighted = $34K on scoping). Right column: AI system with that $34K crossed out and replaced with a fraction. No fixed price shown — just the ratio.
image_brief.composition: Two-column comparison, salary numbers prominent. Strikethrough on the scoping portion. logo bottom-right. Space for hook text at top.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #DC2626 for strikethrough, #10B981 for savings, #FFFFFF text
image_brief.style_reference: Adapt the strikethrough number pattern already proven in batch-002 (200→20, $8K→$5K). Two-column salary comparison.
image_brief.text_overlay: $34K with red strikethrough — max 4 chars on the key number

scenes.environment: Open plan office, mid-morning light. PM character at standing desk with dual monitors showing abstract colour blocks. Papers and sticky notes around the desk — organised chaos.
scenes.action: Typing, then stops, leans back slightly, slight frustrated expression. Picks up a document, flips through it.
scenes.dialogue: I used to spend two full days every week just documenting what clients said in meetings.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, slight orbit right, shallow depth of field

scenes.environment: Same desk, cleaner now. One monitor showing abstract soft blue gradient. The other dark.
scenes.action: Turns to camera, slight forward lean, growing confidence. Tucks hair behind ear.
scenes.dialogue: Now I click one button and the AI pulls the transcript, maps the process, and writes the scope doc.
scenes.camera: Close-up, static, direct eye contact

scenes.environment: Same location, wider angle showing the cleaner desk.
scenes.action: Bright smile, slight head tilt, picks up coffee.
scenes.dialogue: I do actual project management now. Novel concept.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, static hold, slight smile

video_brief.style_reference: Shot on Sony FX3, 35mm f/1.4. Warm, natural office lighting. Empowerment narrative — PM character growing in confidence across scenes.
video_brief.audio: Office ambient — subtle keyboard, coffee machine in background. No music.

angles.source: Market theme: junior empowerment + Hormozi dream outcome: 'replace the process, not the person.' Buying trigger: hiring pain — cost of next PM vs cost of AI system. Agency-specific language gap in competitor ads.

inspiration_ads.why: Clean number-forward layout. Single metric as hero. Adapt for salary comparison format with strikethrough savings.

copy_variants.primary_text: A project manager spends 30-40% of their week on scoping and documentation. At $85K/year, that's $34K on work an AI system can do in hours. How many PMs do you have?
copy_variants.headline: How Many PMs Do You Have?

copy_variants.primary_text: Our PM used to spend 2 days a week turning meeting notes into scope documents. Now she clicks a button and the AI does it in 20 minutes. She does actual project management now. Wild concept.
copy_variants.headline: Free Your PMs From Scoping
copy_variants.description: See what your team could save

copy_variants.primary_text: What's more expensive — your next PM hire at $85K, or a system that handles the scoping half of every PM's job? The math gets uncomfortable fast.
copy_variants.headline: The PM Salary Problem

angles.hook_line: We run 5 client discovery engagements simultaneously. With the same team size we had at 1.

image_brief.scene: Five overlapping client portal cards fanned out in a cascade, each showing a different abstract process map (no readable text — just coloured blocks and flow lines). Clean white background with subtle grid.
image_brief.composition: Cards cascade from top-left to bottom-right. Each card slightly offset. Number '5' large in top-right. logo bottom-right.
image_brief.colour_palette: #FFFFFF background, #1B2A4A card headers, #3B82F6 flow lines, #10B981 status indicators, #F1F5F9 card bodies
image_brief.style_reference: Match the carousel card layout energy of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/3328979794067876.jpg — multiple items in one frame, organised and polished

scenes.environment: Agency boardroom, glass walls showing open-plan office beyond. Late afternoon golden light. Whiteboard behind with abstract sticky note clusters (no readable text). Founder standing.
scenes.action: Walks along the glass wall, gesturing at the team working beyond. Turns to camera mid-stride.
scenes.dialogue: A year ago, one new client engagement meant all hands on deck for two weeks. Now we run five at the same time.
scenes.camera: Tracking medium shot, following the walk. Slight wide angle.

scenes.environment: Same boardroom, seated at table. Laptop open showing abstract blue-green gradient.
scenes.action: Taps laptop, looks at camera with calm authority. Slight lean forward. Counts on fingers — one, two, three, four, five.
scenes.dialogue: Same team. Same overhead. Five times the throughput. Because the AI handles the extraction, the mapping, the documentation.
scenes.camera: Medium close-up, slow push-in

video_brief.style_reference: Shot on Sony FX3, 24mm f/1.4 for the walking scene, 50mm for the seated scene. Warm natural grade. Authority + credibility energy.
video_brief.audio: Subtle open-plan office sounds through the glass — muted conversation, typing. No music.

angles.source: Market theme: agency capacity ceiling. Hormozi dream outcome: 5× throughput without hiring. Whitespace: no competitor addresses scaling concurrent engagements. Social proof: actually does this.

inspiration_ads.why: Carousel/multi-card layout showing scale. Warm palette with organised visual hierarchy. Adapt the multi-element composition for showing 5 concurrent client portals.

copy_variants.primary_text: We run 5 client discovery engagements simultaneously with the same team we had when we could only handle 1. The AI reads every meeting, maps every process, and writes every scope doc. How many could your agency handle?
copy_variants.headline: 5× the Clients. Same Team.
copy_variants.description: See how it scales for you

copy_variants.primary_text: 12 months ago, a new client engagement meant our senior PM was locked up for 2 weeks. Today, she oversees 5 concurrent discoveries. The AI does the heavy lifting. She does the thinking.
copy_variants.headline: From 1 to 5. Same Overhead.
copy_variants.description: Book a call to see how

copy_variants.primary_text: How many client engagements can your agency run at once before someone drops the ball? What if that number was 5× higher — without a single new hire?
copy_variants.headline: What's Your Capacity Ceiling?

angles.hook_line: You're scoping projects for free hoping to win the deal. Here's what that's actually costing you.

image_brief.scene: Hourglass visual with sand (representing money) flowing down. Top half labelled 'Unpaid Scoping Hours', bottom half filling up with dollar signs. Dark background, dramatic lighting.
image_brief.composition: Hourglass centered. Hook text at top. Dollar amount at bottom. logo bottom-right. Clean, minimal — let the metaphor work.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #F59E0B for the sand/money, #DC2626 for the 'unpaid' label, #FFFFFF text
image_brief.style_reference: Adapt the dramatic single-metaphor approach — one powerful visual that tells the story without clutter
image_brief.text_overlay: FREE? in amber at the top of the hourglass — max 5 chars

scenes.environment: Home office at night, desk lamp lighting, laptop open with abstract dark gradient on screen. Coffee mug, slightly dishevelled desk — late working vibes.
scenes.action: Rubbing temples, then looks up at camera. Slight tired expression shifting to knowing look.
scenes.dialogue: Here's the dirty secret of agency life. You scope projects for free. Hoping to win the deal.
scenes.camera: Close-up, warm lamp light, shallow depth of field

scenes.environment: Same desk, slightly brighter — as if the lamp was adjusted. Cleaner framing.
scenes.action: Leans forward, counting on fingers. Eyebrow raise on the word 'thousand'.
scenes.dialogue: Add up the hours. Your senior PM, your tech lead, the meetings, the documents. That's forty, fifty thousand a year in unpaid work.
scenes.camera: Medium close-up, slow push-in

scenes.environment: Same desk, now well-lit. Lamp repositioned. Energy shift — confident.
scenes.action: Sits up straight, slight smile, direct eye contact.
scenes.dialogue: What if scoping took two hours instead of two weeks?
scenes.camera: Close-up, static hold

video_brief.style_reference: Shot on Sony FX3, 50mm f/1.4. Night-to-day lighting shift across scenes = problem-to-solution visual metaphor. BizFlow pain-point storytelling energy.
video_brief.audio: Clock ticking subtly in scene 1 (the pressure). Disappears in scene 3 (the relief). No music.

angles.source: Market theme: the unpaid scoping problem — universal agency pain point. Audience language: 'We scope for free and pray we win the project.' Zero competitors address this in paid ads. Hormozi: agitate the cost of inaction.

inspiration_ads.why: Clean single-concept visual with warm palette. Adapt the minimalist approach for the hourglass/money metaphor. One idea, powerfully executed.

copy_variants.primary_text: Agency scoping is the biggest unpaid job in the industry. Your senior PM spends weeks building proposals and scope docs — for free — hoping to win the deal. We cut that to hours. Plug in your team size and see what you're losing.
copy_variants.headline: Stop Scoping for Free

copy_variants.primary_text: We used to scope projects for free. Two weeks of senior PM time per engagement, win or lose. When we calculated the annual cost, it was more than a full-time salary. So we built a system to fix it.
copy_variants.headline: The Hidden Cost of Scoping

copy_variants.primary_text: How many proposals did you lose last year? Now multiply each one by the hours your team spent scoping it. That's money you'll never get back — unless you change the process.
copy_variants.headline: What Do Lost Proposals Cost?

angles.hook_line: AI made your devs 10× faster. But your scoping process is still manual.

image_brief.scene: Pipeline/funnel diagram. Right side (development) is wide and flowing with speed indicators. Left side (scoping) is a narrow bottleneck with a traffic jam. The constraint is visually obvious.
image_brief.composition: Horizontal pipeline flowing left-to-right. Bottleneck prominent in centre-left. 'SCOPING' label at the bottleneck. 'DEV' label at the wide section. logo bottom-right.
image_brief.colour_palette: #1B2A4A background, #DC2626 for bottleneck, #10B981 for flowing dev section, #FFFFFF text, #3B82F6 for pipeline lines
image_brief.style_reference: Data visualisation style — clean, diagrammatic. Match the authoritative presentation of data/competitor-assets/309796912374932/1827916011114841.jpg
image_brief.text_overlay: SCOPING in red at the bottleneck — max 7 chars

scenes.environment: Modern office, standing at a standing desk. Window light. Laptop on desk showing abstract gradient.
scenes.action: Arms crossed, slight head shake, thoughtful expression. Uncrosses arms and gestures forward.
scenes.dialogue: Everyone's talking about AI for coding. GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude. Your devs are faster than ever.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, static

scenes.environment: Same office, seated now. Slight lean forward.
scenes.action: Eyebrow raise, slight head tilt. Points toward camera on 'you'.
scenes.dialogue: But what's the point of building faster if it takes you two months to figure out what to build?
scenes.camera: Close-up, slow push-in

scenes.environment: Same office, standing again.
scenes.action: Calm smile, open hands gesture (presenting solution).
scenes.dialogue: The bottleneck moved upstream. We fixed it.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, static hold

video_brief.style_reference: Shot on Sony FX3, 35mm f/1.4. Natural office light. Contrarian energy — 'everyone's looking at the wrong thing.' Measured, credible delivery.

angles.source: Market theme #1: the upstream bottleneck — AI accelerated coding but scoping is still manual. This is the 'elephant in the room' angle. Zero competitors address the pre-build phase. Contrarian positioning against the AI-for-coding hype.

inspiration_ads.why: Process/flow visual style. Organised pipeline visualisation. Adapt the clean diagrammatic approach for the bottleneck metaphor.

copy_variants.primary_text: Your developers are 10× faster with AI. But your scoping process hasn't changed in 5 years. The bottleneck moved upstream — and nobody's talking about it. We built a system that scopes in hours, not weeks.
copy_variants.headline: The Bottleneck Moved.
copy_variants.description: See where your time is going

copy_variants.primary_text: We gave our devs Cursor and Claude. Shipping speed doubled. But projects still took months to start — because scoping was manual. So we built an AI system for the OTHER half of the problem.
copy_variants.headline: AI for the Other Half

copy_variants.primary_text: Your developers have Copilot, Cursor, and Claude. What does your scoping team have? If the answer is 'Google Docs and meetings,' you've got a bottleneck that no coding tool will fix.
copy_variants.headline: What About Pre-Build?

angles.angle_name: Try the Output Before You Buy
angles.hook_line: Don't take our word for it. Click through an actual scoping output built by this system.

scenes.dialogue: I could tell you what this system does. Or I could just show you.

scenes.environment: Same desk, monitor now showing abstract blue-green process flow blocks.
scenes.action: Turns back to monitor, clicks (we see his hand on mouse). Turns back to camera, slight nod.
scenes.dialogue: This is a real client portal. Process maps, findings, prototype — all built by the AI from meeting transcripts.

scenes.environment: Same location.
scenes.action: Direct eye contact, casual confidence, slight shrug.
scenes.dialogue: Link's below. Click around. See if it's worth a call.

angles.source: Activation opportunity #3: interactive demo as differentiator. Hormozi: increase perceived likelihood by showing the actual output. Demo-driven conversion theme from market intel. Zero competitors offer try-before-buy.

inspiration_ads.why: Product-forward creative that shows what you get. Clean interface showcase. Adapt the 'show, don't tell' approach for the interactive demo angle.

copy_variants.primary_text: We don't do feature lists. Click the link and try an actual output — process maps, findings reports, and a clickable prototype. All built by AI from client meeting transcripts. See it, then decide if it's worth a call.
copy_variants.headline: Try It. Then Decide.

copy_variants.primary_text: Every agency owner asks the same question: 'Does it actually work?' So we stopped explaining and started showing. Click through a real client portal built entirely by AI. Process maps, findings, prototype — all of it.
copy_variants.headline: See a Real Output

copy_variants.primary_text: When's the last time a software tool let you try the actual output before buying? Click the link, explore a real AI-generated client portal, and decide for yourself.
copy_variants.headline: Try Before You Talk.
